metadata.title: PMDA Guideline on Bioanalytical Method (Ligand Binding Assay) Validation in Pharmaceutical Development
metadata.note: Japan-specific LBA validation guideline. Covers peptide, protein, and low-molecular-weight drug quantification by ligand binding assays (e.g., EIA). Has wider acceptance criteria than the chromatographic BMV guideline (e.g., +/-20% general accuracy vs +/-15%, +/-30% ISR vs +/-20%).

rules.rule_text: A certificate of analysis or alternative statement providing lot number, content (amount, purity, or potency), and storage conditions must accompany the reference standard. Expiration date must be preferably clarified.
rules.verbatim_quote: A certificate of analysis or an alternative statement that provides information on lot number, content (amount, purity, or potency) and storage conditions should accompany the standard. Also, the expiration date or its equivalent is preferably clarified.

rules.rule_text: An analytical method validation must be performed at every relevant facility when establishing a bioanalytical method for quantification of a drug or its metabolite(s).
rules.verbatim_quote: An analytical method validation should be performed at every relevant facility when establishing a bioanalytical method for quantification of a drug or its metabolite(s).

rules.rule_text: A full validation is required when establishing a new LBA method and also when implementing a method from literature or a commercialized kit product. The validation must demonstrate specificity, selectivity, calibration curve, accuracy, precision, dilutional linearity, and stability.
rules.verbatim_quote: A full validation should be performed when establishing a new bioanalytical method for quantification of an analyte/analytes. A full validation is also required when implementing an analytical method that is disclosed in literature or commercialized as a kit product. The objective of a full validation is to demonstrate the assay performance of the method, e.g., specificity, selectivity, calibration curve, accuracy, precision, dilutional linearity, and stability.

rules.rule_text: A full validation must generally be performed for each species or matrix (mainly plasma or serum) to be analyzed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Generally, a full validation should be performed for each species or matrix (mainly plasma or serum) to be analyzed.
rules.exceptions: For rare matrices, a surrogate matrix may be used but must be justified.

rules.rule_text: The minimum required dilution (MRD) must be defined a priori during method development for LBA full validation to dilute samples with buffer solution.
rules.verbatim_quote: In an LBA full validation, the minimum required dilution (MRD) should be defined a priori (i.e., in the course of method development) to dilute samples with buffer solution.

rules.rule_text: For plate-based LBA, analysis must generally be performed in at least 2 wells per sample. Sample concentration must be determined by calculating a mean of responses or averaging concentrations from each response.
rules.verbatim_quote: When using a plate-based LBA, analysis should generally be performed in at least 2 wells per sample; a sample concentration should then be determined either by calculating a mean of responses from the wells or by averaging the concentrations calculated from each response.

rules.rule_text: Specificity must be evaluated using blank samples and blank samples spiked with related substances at anticipated concentrations. QC samples near LLOQ and ULOQ spiked with related substances must demonstrate accuracy within +/-20% (or +/-25% at LLOQ and ULOQ).
rules.verbatim_quote: accuracy in the measurements of the QC samples spiked with the related substance should demonstrate an accuracy of within +/-20% of the theoretical concentration (or within +/-25% of the theoretical concentration at the LLOQ and ULOQ).

rules.rule_text: Selectivity must be evaluated using blank samples obtained from at least 10 individual sources and near-LLOQ QC samples prepared using the individual blank samples.
rules.verbatim_quote: Selectivity is evaluated using blank samples obtained from at least 10 individual sources and near-LLOQ QC samples (i.e., QC samples at or near the LLOQ) prepared using the individual blank samples.
rules.exceptions: In the case of a matrix with limited availability, it may be acceptable to use matrix samples obtained from less than 10 sources.

rules.rule_text: At least 80% of blank samples must have assay results below the LLOQ. At least 80% of near-LLOQ QC samples must demonstrate accuracy within +/-20% (or +/-25% at the LLOQ).
rules.verbatim_quote: Assay results for at least 80% of the blank samples should be below the LLOQ; at least 80% of the near-LLOQ QC samples should demonstrate an accuracy of within +/-20% of the theoretical concentration (or within +/-25% at the LLOQ).

rules.rule_text: The calibration curve must be generated with at least 6 concentration levels of calibration standards including LLOQ and ULOQ samples, and a blank sample. A 4- or 5-parameter logistic model is generally used for the regression equation.
rules.verbatim_quote: A calibration curve should be generated with at least 6 concentration levels of calibration standards, including LLOQ and ULOQ samples, and a blank sample. Anchor point samples at concentrations below the LLOQ and above ULOQ of the calibration curve may also be used to improve curve fitting. A 4- or 5-parameter logistic model is generally used for the regression equation of a calibration curve.

rules.rule_text: Back-calculated concentrations of calibration standards must be within +/-25% at the LLOQ and ULOQ, and within +/-20% at all other levels. At least 75% of standards (excluding anchor points, minimum 6 levels including LLOQ and ULOQ) must meet criteria.
rules.verbatim_quote: The accuracy of back-calculated concentration of each calibration standard should be within +/-25% deviation of the theoretical concentration at the LLOQ and ULOQ, and within +/-20% deviation at all other levels. At least 75% of the calibration standards excluding anchor points, and a minimum of 6 levels of calibration standards, including the LLOQ and ULOQ, should meet the above criteria.

rules.rule_text: QC samples for accuracy/precision must include a minimum of 5 concentrations (LLOQ, low, mid, high, ULOQ). Low-level within 3x LLOQ, mid near midpoint, high at least one-third of ULOQ.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the validation, QC samples with a minimum of 5 different concentrations (LLOQ, low-, mid-, high-levels, and ULOQ) within the calibration range are prepared. The low-level should be within 3 times the LLOQ, the mid-level is near the midpoint on the calibration curve, and the high-level should be at least one-third of the ULOQ of the calibration curve.

rules.rule_text: Accuracy and precision must be evaluated by repeating the analysis in at least 6 analytical runs.
rules.verbatim_quote: Accuracy and precision should be evaluated by repeating the analysis in at least 6 analytical runs.

rules.rule_text: Mean within-run and between-run accuracy must be within +/-20% of theoretical concentration, except at LLOQ and ULOQ where it must be within +/-25%. Precision must not exceed 20% CV (or 25% at LLOQ and ULOQ).
rules.verbatim_quote: The mean within-run and between-run accuracy at each concentration level should be within +/-20% deviation of the theoretical concentration, except at the LLOQ and ULOQ, where it should be within +/-25%. Within-run and between-run precision of concentrations determined at each level should not exceed 20%, except at the LLOQ and ULOQ, where it should not exceed 25%.

rules.rule_text: Total error (sum of absolute value of relative error and precision) must not exceed 30% at each level, except at LLOQ and ULOQ where it must not exceed 40%.
rules.verbatim_quote: Furthermore, a total error (sum of the absolute value of the relative error [i.e., accuracy minus 100%] and precision) at each level should not exceed 30%, except at the LLOQ and ULOQ, where it should not exceed 40%.

rules.rule_text: Dilutional linearity must be evaluated by analyzing a QC sample exceeding the ULOQ and its serial dilutions. Accuracy and precision corrected for dilution factor must be within +/-20% and not more than 20% CV, respectively.
rules.verbatim_quote: Dilutional linearity is evaluated by analyzing a QC sample exceeding the ULOQ of a calibration curve and its serial dilutions at multiple concentrations... Accuracy and precision in the measurements corrected for the dilution factor should be within +/-20% deviation of the theoretical concentration and not more than 20%, respectively.

rules.rule_text: Measures must be taken to eliminate response reduction (hook effect or prozone) in study sample analysis if discovered during dilutional linearity evaluation.
rules.verbatim_quote: The absence or presence of response reduction (hook effect or prozone) is checked in the analyzed samples, and if discovered, measures should be taken to eliminate response reduction in study sample analysis.

rules.rule_text: Analyte stability must be evaluated to ensure concentration is not affected through each step from sample collection to analysis. All stability experiments must be performed on samples stored for longer than the actual storage period.
rules.verbatim_quote: Analyte stability should be evaluated to ensure that the concentration is not affected through each step of the process from the sample collection to the analysis... All stability experiments should be performed on samples that have been stored for a time that is longer than the actual storage period.

rules.rule_text: Validation studies must determine analyte stability after freeze and thaw cycles, short-term storage (room temperature, on ice, or under refrigeration), and long-term storage.
rules.verbatim_quote: Validation studies should determine analyte stability after freeze and thaw cycles, and after short-term (e.g., at room temperature, on ice, or under refrigeration) and long-term storage.

rules.rule_text: Matrix stability must be evaluated using low- and high-level QC samples with at least 3 replicates per concentration level. Mean accuracy must be within +/-20% of theoretical concentration in principle.
rules.verbatim_quote: Stability is evaluated by analysis of at least 3 replicates per QC concentration level before and after stability storage. The mean accuracy in the measurements at each level should be within +/-20% deviation of the theoretical concentration, in principle.
rules.exceptions: Other criteria could be used if they are deemed scientifically more appropriate for the evaluation of a specific analyte.

rules.rule_text: Acceptance criteria used in partial validation must, in principle, be the same as those employed in the full validation.
rules.verbatim_quote: Acceptance criteria used in partial validation should, in principle, be the same as those employed in the full validation.

rules.rule_text: Partial validation is in principle required when the critical reagent lot is changed.
rules.verbatim_quote: Partial validation is in principle required when the critical reagent lot is changed.

rules.rule_text: Cross validation among 2 or more laboratories requires mean accuracy of QC samples (low-, mid-, and high-levels) to be within +/-30% of theoretical concentration. When using study samples, assay variability must be within +/-30% for at least two-thirds.
rules.verbatim_quote: In the cross validation among 2 or more laboratories within a study, the mean accuracy of QC samples (low-, mid-, and high-levels) evaluated by at least 3 replicates at each level, should be within +/-30% deviation of the theoretical concentration, in principle, considering intra- and inter-laboratory precision. When using a set of study samples, the assay variability should be within +/-30% for at least two-thirds of the samples.

rules.rule_text: Study samples must be analyzed using a fully validated analytical method, handled under conditions validated for adequate stability, within the confirmed stability period, with a blank sample, calibration standards at minimum 6 concentration levels, and QC samples at minimum 3 concentration levels.
rules.verbatim_quote: Analysis of study samples should be carried out using a fully validated analytical method. During analysis, study samples should be handled under conditions that are validated for adequate stability, and analyzed within a confirmed stability period, along with a blank sample, calibration standards at a minimum of 6 concentration levels, and QC samples at a minimum of 3 concentration levels.

rules.rule_text: In plate-based LBA, each plate represents a single analytical run. Assay must be performed in at least 2 wells per sample.
rules.verbatim_quote: In a plate-based assay, each plate represents a single analytical run. In a plate-based LBA, assay should generally be performed in at least 2 wells per sample prepared.

rules.rule_text: Calibration standard back-calculated concentrations during study sample analysis must be within +/-25% at LLOQ and ULOQ, and +/-20% at other levels. At least 75% of standards (excluding anchor points, minimum 6 levels) must meet criteria.
rules.verbatim_quote: The accuracy of back-calculated concentrations of calibration standards at each level should be within +/-25% deviation of the theoretical concentration at the LLOQ and ULOQ of the calibration curve, and +/-20% deviation at all other levels. At least 75% of the calibration standards excluding anchor points, with a minimum of 6 levels, should meet the above criteria.
rules.exceptions: If the calibration standard at the LLOQ or ULOQ does not meet criteria, the next lowest/highest-level standard may be used as the LLOQ or ULOQ.

rules.rule_text: QC samples during study sample analysis must include minimum 3 levels (low, mid, high). Low-level within 3x LLOQ, mid at midrange, high at least one-third of ULOQ. Two QC samples at each level or 5% of total study samples are required, whichever is greater.
rules.verbatim_quote: QC samples with a minimum of 3 different concentration levels (low-, mid-, and high-levels) within the calibration range are analyzed in each analytical run... The analysis requires 2 QC samples at each QC level or at least 5% of the total number of study samples in the analytical run, whichever is the greater.

rules.rule_text: QC sample accuracy during study sample analysis must be within +/-20% of theoretical concentrations. At least two-thirds of QC samples and at least 50% at each concentration level must meet criteria.
rules.verbatim_quote: The accuracy of measurement of QC samples should be within +/-20% deviation of the theoretical concentrations. At least two-thirds of the QC samples and at least 50% at each concentration level should meet the above criterion.

rules.rule_text: ISR must be performed for representative studies using PK data as primary endpoint: non-clinical toxicokinetic studies for each different species, representative clinical PK studies in healthy volunteers and patients with renal/hepatic impairment, and bioequivalence studies.
rules.verbatim_quote: ISR should be conducted in the following situations: non-clinical toxicokinetic studies for each different species, representative clinical pharmacokinetic studies in healthy volunteers and patients with renal/hepatic impairment, as well as bioequivalence studies.
rules.exceptions: For non-clinical studies, ISR may be performed with samples from an independent non-GLP study if the study design is similar.

rules.rule_text: ISR samples must include those near Cmax and the elimination phase. Approximately 10% of samples must be reanalyzed when total is less than 1000, and approximately 5% when exceeding 1000.
rules.verbatim_quote: ISR should be performed with samples from as many subjects or animals as possible, including those near the maximum blood concentration (Cmax) and the elimination phase, within a time window that ensures the analyte stability. As a guide, approximately 10% of the samples should be reanalyzed in cases where the total number of study samples is less than 1000 and approximately 5% of the number of samples exceeding 1000.

rules.rule_text: ISR assay variability must be within +/-30% for at least two-thirds of the samples analyzed (wider tolerance than chromatographic methods). Root cause investigation is required if criteria are not met.
rules.verbatim_quote: The assay variability should be within +/-30% for at least two-thirds of the samples analyzed in ISR. In case the ISR data failed to meet the above criteria, root cause investigation should be conducted for the analytical method and necessary measures should be taken by considering the potential impact on study sample analysis.

rules.rule_text: Original ISR data must never be discarded or replaced with reanalysis data even if assay variability exceeds +/-30% for a specific sample.
rules.verbatim_quote: The original data should never be discarded or replaced with the reanalysis data even if the assay variability exceeds +/-30% in a specific sample.

rules.rule_text: In bioequivalence studies, reanalysis of study samples for PK reasons is not acceptable. Reanalysis is only acceptable when initial analysis yields unexpected or anomalous results that may affect subject safety.
rules.verbatim_quote: Particularly in bioequivalence studies, it is not acceptable to reanalyze study samples and modify the concentration data only because the initial data were pharmacokinetically questionable. However, reanalysis of specific study samples is acceptable when, for instance, the initial analysis yielded an unexpected or anomalous result that may affect the safety of subject in a clinical trial.

rules.rule_text: Critical reagents (e.g., binding reagents such as unlabeled or labeled antibodies) must be selected considering specificity for the analyte, stored under conditions ensuring consistent quality, and their quality maintained throughout method validation and study sample analysis.
rules.verbatim_quote: A critical reagent should be selected by considering the specificity for the analyte and should be stored under conditions that ensure consistent quality. The quality of critical reagent should be appropriately maintained throughout the period of use in analytical method validation and study sample analysis.

rules.rule_text: All relevant records and raw data must be kept including those from rejected analytical runs, specifically records of reference materials, blank matrices, critical reagents, samples, instruments, deviations, communications, and raw analytical data.
rules.verbatim_quote: All relevant records and raw data should be kept, including those obtained in rejected analytical runs, specifically record of reference materials, blank matrices, and critical reagents (receipt/release, use, and storage), record of samples (receipt/release, preparation, and storage), record of analyses, record of instrument (calibration and settings), record of deviations, record of communications, and raw data such as analytical data.
